root_cause_taxonomy.spec_gap: spec silent on something implementation needed
root_cause_taxonomy.spec_contradiction: two spec passages disagreed; implementation surfaced it
root_cause_taxonomy.silent_divergence: code deviated from spec without a spec edit
root_cause_taxonomy.test_blindspot: tests structurally unable to catch the defect
root_cause_taxonomy.unrecorded_deferral: scope narrowed without recording it (honesty, not correctness)
root_cause_taxonomy.design_bug: reachable incorrect behavior within a single component
root_cause_taxonomy.memory_authored_config: version or config written from memory instead of registry/tool
root_cause_taxonomy.unverified_claim: a claim about a repo file, command, or config asserted without reading the source of truth
root_cause_taxonomy.process_gap: missing workflow mechanism (tracking, single entry point)

findings.summary: OSS-6 part 2 (cross-version eval comparison) silently undischargeable in Phase 0
findings.fix: explicit it.todo pointing at Phase 2

findings.summary: plan promised rebuildIndex-from-files; only rehashFromDisk shipped (needs Phase 1 manifest for upstreams)
findings.fix: scope note in plan doc + task notes; discharged with DSL-6

findings.summary: test silently reworded obligation (ingestion vs emit-on-session-end) without recording the deferral
findings.fix: it.todo for transcript parse + emit trigger (P0-7)

findings.summary: Phase 0 stubs existed only as code comments: sdlc_step=build, effort=medium, cost=0 (indistinguishable from measured zero), one never-transitioned open task per session inflating counts
findings.fix: recorded here: stage/effort attribution and unit prices land Phase 3; real task linkage lands with the CLI task store

findings.summary: TEL-6 metrics half (north-star/secondary as OTel metrics) unimplemented — traces/spans only
findings.fix: deferral recorded here: metrics export lands when FPAR/TPAC aggregation queries exist as kernel functions

findings.summary: Both blind readers committed to all-or-nothing promotion (invalid id rejects the whole batch, non-zero exit) but promoteInferred silently skips non-inferred ids — partial promotion of a curated selection; F-120 class: agreed reading, unimplemented surface
findings.fix: UX-22 pins strict all-or-nothing; promoteInferred gains validate-then-throw (no mutation on a bad batch); collapsed module counts pinned authority-split (reader B)

findings.summary: UX-26 readers converged on git-index enumeration and unrestricted deletion of unregenerable rows; kernel has no git dependency and opaque artifact rows cannot be classified as file-gone, so unrestricted deletion is destructive
findings.fix: UX-26 re-pinned: filesystem scan (no git), deletion confined to provably spec-derived rows (logical_id naming a *.spec.md source), full count semantics pinned

findings.summary: doctor store probe used openDb, which creates the missing store and reports it passing — a diagnostic that mutates state and misdirects a fresh clone
findings.fix: UX-19 pinned read-only probing (missing store fails naming kelson init, never created); existsSync guard + missing-store fixture

findings.summary: duplicate-id dedupe pinned in UX-22 but untested; drift list survival threshold 0 undeclared vs SPEC-8 default 20
findings.fix: UX-22 obligation gains the duplicate-id case (test added); clause pins list threshold 0 with --min-sessions filter

findings.summary: a runTask/createWorkspace failure left the replay eval_run row with finished_at NULL — forever-running in the eval view
findings.fix: UX-23 pins finished_at stamping on failure; runReplay stamps in finally; missing-snapshot fixture added

findings.summary: docs/guides/brownfield.md still said drift promote ships with the TUI — stale after this diff shipped it as a static command
findings.fix: guide line updated to name kelson drift list/promote

findings.summary: board.mjs task create reused archived-completed id C1-11, reviving it and nulling completed_at — create path never checked the completed/archived namespace
findings.fix: restored C1-11 from HEAD into archive; --title now dies on any existing id, active or archived

findings.summary: stamp-head's added-line diff heuristic assumed an append-only findings.json; the archive rewrite repositioned old rows so open F-096 matched as newly-added and got a fix_commit while unfixed
findings.fix: reset F-096 fix_commit to null; stamp-head now stamps only status=fixed rows

findings.summary: LOOP-4 protected enumeration gained the edit budget in spec while PROTECTED_TARGETS and the LOOP-4 obligation matrix were unchanged — spec edit shipped without its paired code/test on a false unbuilt-Phase-4 premise
findings.fix: PROTECTED_TARGETS gained edit-budget; LOOP-4 matrix extended to 5 targets in the same change

findings.summary: LOOP-11 mandated a required rejections_seen_through field while the shipped strict Proposal schema had no such field — every proposal the live implementation emitted would be spec-non-conformant; no obligation test, no ERD ripple
findings.fix: schema field + migration 0011 + createProposal required arg + assembleRejectionHistory/emitProposalCycle + LOOP-10/LOOP-11 obligation tests + ERD field note in the same change

findings.summary: rename left seed benchmark snapshots stale: bundles still materialized docs/kelspec/*.spec.md with kelspec fences while the renamed compiler reads docs/obspec + obspec fences, so spec-repair-vague-clause (obligations = its only check, gating suite) degraded to a vacuous auto-pass; greenfield-rate-limiter statement pointed at the dead path. Gates could not catch it: snapshots are opaque content-addressed git bundles, obspec-lint sweeps checked-out spec files only, no gate executes the seed suite
findings.fix: both snapshots regenerated (clone, git mv docs/kelspec->docs/obspec, fence+title+prose rewrite with per-step count asserts, re-bundle, re-hash, restore-verify), task.yaml snapshot refs + statements updated, old bundles removed

findings.summary: writeOverlay assumed ~/.obligato exists and threw ENOENT on a never-configured HOME — both the new openai-compatible login and the existing ollama login crashed on a fresh machine; obligation tests never saw it because makeTestRepo pre-creates home/.obligato
findings.fix: mkdirSync(recursive) in writeOverlay (single choke point for both providers); PROV-11 test case (a) now rmSyncs the pre-created dir so a revert fails the suite; caught by the operator-surface E2E, not the fixture suite

findings.summary: pre-commit stamp-head mis-stamped F-150..F-155 with the rename commit sha: the .kelson->.obligato path rename made its added-line heuristic read every existing row as new — F-169 diff-shape class recurring on the renamed board file, corrupting fix_commit provenance the postmortem/LOOP-6 miners read
findings.fix: six rows restamped to their true fix commit 402fb5d via board.mjs stamp; stamp-head rename-robustness queued as its own task (C1-20)

findings.summary: the F-174 restamp silently never landed: board.mjs stamp only wrote rows whose fix_commit was null, so correcting a mis-stamp no-opd on all six rows while printing the success line — the staged diff then carried a false fix claim (F-174 text said restamped; the same file disproved it). The repo gotcha class exactly: a scripted mutation must verify its own effect
findings.fix: stamp now treats explicitly named ids as authoritative: overwrites, reports each old->new correction, and re-loads to assert every named row carries the sha (dies otherwise); restamp re-run and verified in worktree and index

findings.summary: CI merge-base checks (pack version-bump lint, changelog append-only) were broken on their primary trigger: actions/checkout depth-1 has no common ancestor between a PR head and origin/main, so git merge-base printed nothing and the scripts died on usage — never seen before because the repo had never raised a PR (push-to-main degenerates merge-base to HEAD, vacuously passing)
findings.fix: fetch-depth: 0 on the checks job checkout; surfaced and verified by PR #1, the repo first PR
